original_hypothesis.title: Forced Hook Execution Causes O(N) Gas Griefing on Transfers
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: When a market requires withdrawal access, the hook contract forcibly enables the `Bit_Enabled_Transfer` flag. This causes the market to call `onTransfer` for every token transfer. Even if `transferRequiresAccess` is false, the hook still calls `_tryValidateAccessInner` for any recipient who is not a known lender. If the recipient lacks a credential, this triggers `_loopTryGetCredential`, making an external `staticcall` to EVERY registered pull provider. An attacker can exploit this by transferring 1 wei to many uncredentialed addresses, or the sheer baseline gas cost of multiple external calls could break DEX composability.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: Forced execution of credential validation loops on unrestricted transfers creates massive, unintended gas overhead.

proof_of_signal.pattern: _tryValidateAccessInner called even when transferRequiresAccess is false

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: O(N) external calls per transfer where N is number of pull providers
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Network congestion griefing or breaking competitor DEX pools

economic_questions: What is the gas cost per transfer with 5-10 pull providers?
Does this exceed standard DEX router gas limits?
validation_requirements: Measure gas consumption of onTransfer with multiple pull providers
Test integration with Uniswap V2/V3 routers

investigation_priorities.parameter_analysis: Gas cost scaling with pull provider count

validation_result.mechanism: O(N) Gas Griefing on Unrestricted Transfers
validation_result.impact_summary: Unrestricted token transfers incur O(N) external calls to all registered pull providers, causing massive gas overhead and potentially breaking DEX composability.
validation_result.detailed_analysis: When a market is deployed with `useOnQueueWithdrawal` enabled, the hook contract forcibly enables the `Bit_Enabled_Transfer` flag, meaning `onTransfer` is called for every token transfer. In `onTransfer`, if the recipient is not already a known lender, the contract calls `_tryValidateAccessInner` to check if they have a valid credential. This is done even if `transferRequiresAccess` is false, with the intent of opportunistically upgrading the recipient to a 'known lender'. However, if the recipient does not have a credential (which is expected for unrestricted transfers to smart contracts, DEX pools, or regular users), `_tryValidateAccessInner` falls back to `_loopTryGetCredential`. This function iterates over every registered pull provider and makes an external `staticcall` to each one. As a result, every normal transfer to a new address incurs O(N) external calls, where N is the number of pull providers. This creates a massive, unintended gas overhead that can break composability with DEX routers (which often have strict gas limits) and allows attackers to grief the network or specific protocols by initiating transfers that consume excessive gas.
validation_result.code_evidence_assessment: The code explicitly documents the intent to validate access even when not required, but fails to account for the unbounded gas cost of looping through all pull providers when the user lacks a credential.

vulnerable_code_snippets.vulnerability_explanation: The contract calls `_tryValidateAccessInner` regardless of whether `market.transferRequiresAccess` is true. If the user has no credential, this triggers a loop over all pull providers, consuming significant gas for an unrestricted transfer.

This loop makes an external `staticcall` via `_tryGetCredential` for every registered pull provider. When called during an unrestricted transfer to an uncredentialed address, it will execute the full loop, maximizing gas consumption.

execution_path_analysis.vulnerable_code_path: When a token transfer occurs, the market calls `onTransfer`. If `isKnownLenderOnMarket[to][msg.sender]` is false, `_tryValidateAccessInner` is invoked. If the recipient has no prior credential, `_tryValidateAccessInner` calls `_loopTryGetCredential`, which iterates through all `_pullProviders`. For each provider, `_tryGetCredential` makes an external `staticcall`. Since the recipient has no credential, all calls fail/return false, and the loop completes. Finally, `onTransfer` checks `market.transferRequiresAccess`; since it is false, the transfer succeeds, but the transaction has been inflated by O(N) external calls.

validation_result.attack_scenario_validation: An attacker can exploit this by transferring dust amounts (e.g., 1 wei) of market tokens to many uncredentialed addresses (or a target smart contract). Each transfer forces the hook to iterate over all pull providers, consuming excessive gas. This can be used to grief network block space, drain gas-sponsoring relayers, or break composability for legitimate users trying to swap market tokens on a DEX, as the gas cost of the transfer will exceed standard router limits.
validation_result.impact_assessment: High. The unintended gas overhead degrades the transferability of the market token, potentially breaking integrations with DEXes and other smart contracts. It also introduces a cheap gas griefing vector.
recommended_mitigations: Only call `_tryValidateAccessInner` in `onTransfer` if `market.transferRequiresAccess` is true, OR if `extraData` is explicitly provided indicating the user is actively trying to present a credential.
If opportunistic credential checking is strictly required, limit it to checking existing cached credentials rather than triggering the expensive `_loopTryGetCredential` pull mechanism.
validation_result.replication_plan: 1. Deploy a market with `useOnQueueWithdrawal` = true and `useOnTransfer` = false. 2. Add 10 pull providers to the hook contract. 3. Measure the gas cost of transferring 1 wei of market tokens to a new, uncredentialed address. 4. Observe that the gas cost is inflated by 10 external staticcalls compared to a standard transfer.
additional_testing_needed: Measure exact gas consumption of `onTransfer` with varying numbers of pull providers (e.g., 1, 5, 10, 50).
Test token transfers through standard Uniswap V2/V3 router contracts to verify if the inflated gas cost causes out-of-gas reverts during swaps.
